Hero.title: Preventive urban sanitary infrastructure
Hero.subtitle: A system integrated into sidewalks and curbs for early detection and preventive control of urban pests.

Surface.title: Can the urban surface be protected?
Surface.text: The problem is real. Cities have spent decades operating on two layers: underground and inside buildings. But there is a third layer where pests emerge, move and find food: <bold>the urban surface.</bold>

Product.label: About
Product.title: What is it?
Product.desc1: XANAEL is a preventive urban sanitary infrastructure integrated into the city's road network.
Product.desc2: On the outside, it is part of the urban curb.
Product.desc2b: Inside, it features a technical chamber designed for early detection, monitoring and control of urban pests.
Product.benefit1Desc: Fixed intervention points integrated into the urban road network. They enable early detection, monitoring and control when needed.
Product.benefit2Desc: It is part of the city's physical design. It is not a portable device: it is permanent sanitary infrastructure.
Product.a1:
Urban rodents follow habitual routes along curbs and sidewalks.

XANAEL intercepts these routes by integrating detection and control points directly into the curb.

This allows detection and action before the problem reaches buildings.
Product.a2:
Municipalities, industries, pest control companies and all types of spaces with outdoor road networks.

Shopping centres, schools, theme parks, hotels, public parks, swimming pools or industrial complexes where there are streets, sidewalks or surface transit areas.

Manifesto.text: The city can also protect itself. XANAEL integrates pest prevention directly into urban infrastructure. Permanent, discreet and without invasive maintenance.

Infrastructure.heroSubtitle: On the outside, an urban curb. On the inside, sanitary infrastructure designed for pest detection and control.
Infrastructure.feat1Text: Integrates directly into the urban road network, not as an add-on element
Infrastructure.feat2Text: Compatible with various pest detection, monitoring and control systems
Infrastructure.feat3Text: Standard construction element with no structural maintenance required
Infrastructure.feat4Text: First surface-level preventive pest infrastructure system integrated into urban areas
Infrastructure.problem1: The urban surface had no dedicated preventive infrastructure
Infrastructure.problem2: Traditional pest control only acts once the problem has already appeared
Infrastructure.problem3: Without physical infrastructure, prevention depends on external interventions
Infrastructure.problem4: Buildings and facilities are left exposed from the urban perimeter
Infrastructure.problem5: Integrated pest management (GIP/IPM) lacked a surface-level control layer
Infrastructure.compactDesc: Precast concrete technical curb designed to be integrated into sidewalks, building perimeters and urban road networks. It features an accessible interior chamber with a removable technical tray, ready to integrate early detection, monitoring and pest control systems.
Infrastructure.standardDesc: Larger precast concrete technical curb, designed for installation on larger perimeters and high-demand areas.
Infrastructure.install1: It is installed just like any other urban curb element.
Infrastructure.install2: Once placed, no additional construction work is required.
Infrastructure.install3: The interior chamber is accessible from the top cover for inspection, monitoring or control tasks.
Infrastructure.install4: It is installed during road construction or by replacing existing curbs.
Infrastructure.tapa1: Each XANAEL curb includes a certified EN124 class B125 cast iron cover.
Infrastructure.tapa2: The central emblem can be customised with the municipality's or organisation's coat of arms, reinforcing its integration into the public space.
Infrastructure.tapa3: This allows XANAEL to become a visible part of the municipality's urban furniture.
Infrastructure.adv1: First surface-level preventive pest infrastructure system integrated into urban areas
Infrastructure.adv2: Compatible with any pest control company
Infrastructure.adv3: It does not replace existing systems: it complements them
Infrastructure.adv4: One-time installation, no subsequent structural interventions
Infrastructure.adv5: Aligned with integrated pest management (GIP/IPM) under European regulations
Infrastructure.adv6: Registered access exclusively for authorised technicians
Infrastructure.formSubtitle: Fill in the form and our team will provide you with information about deployment, distribution or collaboration with XANAEL.

Infrastructure.formSent: Form submitted successfully. We will get in touch with you.

About.title: About us
About.intro: We integrate pest prevention into urban infrastructure. Directly into the curb.
About.originText1: XANAEL was born within Grupo Rubio Servicios Higiénicos Integrales — a company with over 25 years of experience in pest control, environmental management and hygienic treatments.
About.originText2: The founders — Carlos, Iñaki and José Javier Rubio Carrera — identified a structural gap: cities lack physical surface-level infrastructure to prevent and control pests before they access buildings.
About.originText3: From that premise, XANAEL was designed and patented: a precast concrete technical curb that integrates an accessible interior chamber, ready for early detection, monitoring and control of urban pests.
About.innovationText1: XANAEL introduces a new concept in urban environmental health: preventive infrastructure integrated at the surface level.
About.innovationText2: The system is patented in Spain and is installed like a conventional curb. No additional construction work is required. Its interior chamber allows the integration of pest detection, monitoring and control systems without altering the urban environment.
About.innovationText3: It is compatible with the integrated pest management (GIP/IPM) required by European regulations and can operate alongside any company in the sector.
About.teamText: The XANAEL team presented the system at EXPOCIDA 2026, the leading environmental health congress in Spain. The concept was validated there before manufacturers, pest control companies and municipal authorities.
